articles.title: Dutch prime minister says vital to show Iran 'breaches of ceasefire' unacceptable
articles.summary: Dutch prime minister says vital to show Iran 'breaches of ceasefire' unacceptable Dutch Prime Minister Rob Jetten said that it was important to show Iran that "breaches" of the truce in the Middle East will not be tolerated. When asked about the latest US attacks on Iran, Jetten told reporters "it is important to show you don't accept breaches of the ceasefire". "At the same time, you must apply maximum diplomatic pressure to make sure talks continue and a solution is reached," he added.

articles.summary: Iran claims drone strike on US forces in Bahraini air base The Iranian army announced it launched a drone attack on US forces at the Isa Air Base in Bahrain, vowing that Washington's army infrastructure in the region will all be "legitimate targets". "The consequences of the flagrant and repeated violation and breaking of the ceasefire with America are criminal, and all American bases in the region will be legitimate targets of military drones," it said.

articles.title: Nato chief says US attacks on Iran 'absolutely necessary'
articles.summary: Nato chief says US attacks on Iran 'absolutely necessary' Fresh US strikes on Iran were "absolutely necessary", Nato Secretary General Mark Rutte told reporters during a key summit in Turkey's Ankara.  "I think it was absolutely necessary because when you have a ceasefire and Iran is basically violating the ceasefire... I think it is totally crucial that the US forcefully react," he said.

articles.summary: In pictures: Funeral procession for Iran's slain supreme leader continues in Iraq's Najaf The funeral procession for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei continued on in the Iraqi city of Najaf on Wednesday, amid fresh US attacks in Iran.  His burial is expected to be held on Thursday in the north east Iranian city of Mashhad. Crowds gather to mourn Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Najaf, Iraq, 8 July, 2026 (Alaa Al-Marjani/Reuters) The coffin of Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on February 28 in Israeli and US air strike

articles.title: IRGC says it downed a US drone, struck infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain
articles.summary: IRGC says it downed a US drone, struck infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said they struck dozens of US military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait in response to Washington's attacks south of the country.  "In an initial response to this aggression, the IRGC Navy and Aerospace Force carried out a joint missile and drone operation, striking 85 key US military facilities," the statement carried by state broadcaster IRIB read.  It added that an MQ-9 drone was struck down in southern Iran.

articles.summary: Iran's Ghalibaf slams renewed US attacks, Israeli violations in Lebanon Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf accused the US of "major [Memorandum of Understanding] violations", following renewed US attacks south of the country.  Ghalibaf said that the violations include continued threats of further strikes, reinstating oil sanctions, hits on southern Iran as well as continued Israeli attacks on Lebanon.  "The era of bullying and extortion is over. articles.title: Sirens sound in Bahrain, Kuwait
articles.summary: Sirens sound in Bahrain, Kuwait Air sirens were activated in Bahrain, the country's interior ministry said.  "Citizens and residents are urged to remain calm and head to the nearest safe place," the ministry said on X. Meanwhile, the Kuwaiti army indicated that it was responding to "hostile missile and drone attacks". It added that the General Staff of the Army noted "that any explosions heard are a result of air defense systems intercepting hostile attacks".

articles.title: Iran's top joint military command vows 'crushing response' against US
articles.summary: Iran's top joint military command vows 'crushing response' against US Iran's top joint military command, Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, said that the country's armed forces would deliver a "crushing response" against the US following attacks on southern Iran.  The statement described the strikes as a "blatant act of aggression", adding that Tehran would not allow US intervention in the management of the Strait of Hormuz.  It stressed that the only safe route for vessels through the waterway was the passage set up by Iran.
